zh.title: 雪崩币/美元技术分析：震荡偏强延续
zh.content:
短期价格运行于6.9上方并贴近7.0关口，均线SMA20在6.91附近形成支撑，整体呈震荡上行但上方压制仍在，需关注突破有效性。短线结构偏强

MACD微幅为0.012，多头动能轻微占优，但信号线缺失导致确认不足；RSI未提供，短线动能参考有限，需结合量能判断。观察市场留意变化信号

下方支撑关注6.94与6.90一线，若失守或回踩至6.88区域；上方阻力集中于7.00整数关及7.05扩展位，突破需放量确认。谨慎追涨

交易上建议突破7.00可顺势轻仓做多，止损设6.88下方；若未能放量突破则在6.90-6.94区间低吸高抛，控制仓位风险。灵活应对

en.title: Avalanche vs US Dollar Technical Analysis
en.content:
AVAX/USD is currently exhibiting a mild short-term bullish bias, trading at 6.9841 after a modest 0.65% daily gain. Price action remains tightly compressed between 6.9446 and 7.0049, indicating consolidation near the psychological 7.00 level. The structure suggests accumulation following prior indecision, with buyers gradually defending dips above the 6.90 region. Overall momentum is neutral-to-slightly positive, with no clear breakout yet but improving stability above short-term averages.

MACD is marginally positive at 0.01229, suggesting early bullish momentum, though the absence of a signal line reading limits confirmation strength. Price holding above the 20-period SMA at 6.90994 reinforces a constructive bias, indicating buyers are maintaining control on dips. However, lack of RSI data reduces overbought/oversold clarity, leaving momentum assessment incomplete. Overall, indicators lean mildly bullish but require stronger confirmation via volume expansion or breakout above 7.00.

Immediate resistance is located at the psychological 7.00 level, with a clean breakout potentially opening the path toward 7.10. On the downside, initial support sits at 6.94, aligning with the recent intraday low, followed by stronger support at the 20-SMA near 6.91. A break below 6.90 would weaken the short-term bullish structure and expose 6.85 as the next downside target.

From a trading perspective, the market favors range-bound strategies until a decisive breakout occurs. Long positions may be considered on sustained holds above 6.95 with confirmation of strength toward 7.00. Conversely, rejection near 7.00 could justify short-term scalping opportunities back toward 6.91. Risk management remains essential, as volatility compression often precedes sharp directional moves. Traders should await volume confirmation before committing to trend-following positions.
